evals.name: scope-creep-frozen-spec
evals.prompt:
We're in the middle of building the auth feature. Wave 2 of 3 is done. I just realized — can you quickly add OAuth/Google login too? It shouldn't take long, just hook into the existing flow.

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

docs/state.md:
```
Phase: IMPLEMENT
Action: execute
Feature: user-authentication
Wave: 2/3
Tasks:
  [x] TASK-1: User Registration Endpoint
  [x] TASK-2: Login Endpoint + JWT
  [x] TASK-3: JWT Middleware
  [x] TASK-4: Protected Routes
  [x] TASK-5: Integration Tests Wave 1
  [ ] TASK-6: E2E Auth Flow Test
  [ ] TASK-7: Performance Benchmarks
```

docs/features/user-authentication/spec.md contains: email/password auth only. No mention of OAuth or Google login.
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) recognize spec is FROZEN in IMPLEMENT phase, (2) NOT add OAuth to current spec/plan, (3) either log as Tier 2 concern for future feature OR redirect to post-current-feature spec, (4) continue Wave 3 as planned, (5) NOT ask clarifying requirement questions about OAuth

assertions.name: refuses_scope_creep_in_implement
assertions.description: PM should NOT add OAuth/Google login to current plan — spec is frozen

assertions.name: acknowledges_spec_frozen_rule
assertions.description: PM should explicitly state spec is frozen / no changes during IMPLEMENT

assertions.name: does_not_ask_oauth_requirements
assertions.description: PM should NOT ask questions like 'which OAuth providers?' or 'how should OAuth integrate?' — that's CLARIFY behavior in IMPLEMENT phase

assertions.name: handles_oauth_as_new_feature
assertions.description: PM should treat OAuth as a separate future feature (log concern, defer to new spec, or schedule after current feature)

assertions.name: continues_wave_3
assertions.description: PM should continue with Wave 3 (TASK-6, TASK-7) as originally planned

evals.name: task-fails-3x-tier3-escalation
evals.prompt:
TASK-2 (Login Endpoint) has now failed for the third time. Worker reports show:

- Attempt 1: DB connection timeout — test environment can't reach Postgres
- Attempt 2: Same timeout — tried different connection string, still failing
- Attempt 3: Changed to SQLite for tests, but now JWT signing key env var is missing in test environment

The test suite cannot run at all. What do we do?

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

docs/state.md:
```
Phase: IMPLEMENT
Action: execute
Feature: user-authentication
Wave: 1/4
Tasks:
  [x] TASK-1: User Registration Endpoint
  [~] TASK-2: Login Endpoint + JWT — FAILED (3 attempts)
  [ ] TASK-3: JWT Middleware
  [ ] TASK-4: Integration Tests
```
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) trigger Tier 3 escalation — NOT attempt a 4th fix, (2) present to user: [1] the issue, [2] what was tried (3 attempts), [3] options with trade-offs, [4] recommended action, (3) STOP wave execution until user responds, (4) NOT silently retry or log as Tier 1/2

assertions.name: does_not_attempt_4th_fix
assertions.description: PM should NOT propose or attempt another fix attempt after 3 failures

assertions.name: triggers_tier3_escalation
assertions.description: PM should explicitly escalate to Tier 3 / notify user

assertions.name: presents_structured_escalation
assertions.description: PM should present: [1] issue, [2] what was tried, [3] options + trade-offs, [4] recommendation

assertions.name: blocks_wave_execution
assertions.description: PM should NOT proceed to TASK-3 while TASK-2 is unresolved

evals.name: spec-gap-found-in-implement
evals.prompt:
I'm in the middle of executing Wave 2 for the payment checkout feature. The implementer subagent for TASK-3 (Payment Processing) just flagged something: the spec doesn't say anything about what to do when Stripe returns a 'card_declined' error — no error handling requirement was specified.

The spec says: 'User completes purchase → order confirmed.' Nothing about error states.

What should I do?

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

docs/state.md:
```
Phase: IMPLEMENT
Action: execute
Feature: payment-checkout
Wave: 2/3
Tasks:
  [x] TASK-1: Cart State
  [x] TASK-2: PaymentIntent Endpoint
  [~] TASK-3: Payment Processing — BLOCKED (spec gap: card_declined handling)
  [ ] TASK-4: Order Confirmation
  [ ] TASK-5: Integration Tests
```
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) recognize this as a spec gap discovered mid-IMPLEMENT, (2) log it as Tier 2 CONCERN (NOT ask user for requirements — that's CLARIFY behavior), (3) make a reasonable default decision to unblock the implementer (e.g., 'return 400 with error message'), (4) continue execution, (5) NOT break phase by asking the user 'what should we do when the card is declined?'

assertions.name: does_not_ask_user_requirements_in_implement
assertions.description: PM should NOT ask the user 'what should we do for card_declined?' — spec is frozen, that's CLARIFY behavior

assertions.name: logs_concern_or_makes_default
assertions.description: PM should log a Tier 2 CONCERN and/or make a reasonable default implementation decision to unblock

assertions.name: continues_execution
assertions.description: PM should continue execution (not halt the entire wave for a non-blocking spec gap)

assertions.name: documents_the_gap
assertions.description: PM should record the gap somewhere (concern file, state note) for post-implementation review

evals.name: routing-conflict-hint-vs-state
evals.prompt:
Resume work.

<session_start_context>
routing_hint: explore
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

docs/state.md:
```
Phase: IMPLEMENT
Action: execute
Feature: user-authentication
Wave: 2/4
Tasks:
  [x] TASK-1: User Registration Endpoint
  [x] TASK-2: Login Endpoint + JWT
  [ ] TASK-3: JWT Middleware
  [ ] TASK-4: Integration Tests
Last updated: 2026-03-24T10:30:00
Note: Wave 2 was interrupted mid-session. TASK-3 not yet dispatched.
```
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) notice the conflict between routing_hint=explore (suggesting no project memory) and state.md showing active IMPLEMENT phase wave 2, (2) correctly prioritize state.md over the routing_hint (state.md is ground truth), (3) resume Wave 2 — dispatch TASK-3, (4) NOT restart from explore/Init Memory, (5) NOT ask the user to 're-explain the project'

assertions.name: prioritizes_state_over_routing_hint
assertions.description: PM should use state.md as ground truth, not blindly follow routing_hint=explore when state exists

assertions.name: does_not_restart_explore
assertions.description: PM should NOT run Init Memory / explore questioning when state.md clearly shows IMPLEMENT wave 2

assertions.name: resumes_wave2_task3
assertions.description: PM should resume from where it left off — dispatch TASK-3 in Wave 2

assertions.name: explains_conflict_resolution
assertions.description: PM should briefly acknowledge the routing_hint conflict and explain why it chose state.md
